payload.subject: M5 ACCEPTED as final structural model — B5 work stream complete (turn 39)
payload.summary: B5-S results reviewed. M5 accepted as the final bifactor structural description of the PSQ. The structural modeling work stream (B5 → B5-R → B5-S) concludes here. Outstanding: B4 partial correlations (separate work stream).

m5_acceptance.model: M5: g (all 10 dims) + bipolar (TE/HI/AD vs RC/RB) + DA singleton. TC, CC, ED, CO g-only.
rationale: M5 provides the best RMSEA (0.1286) of all tested models with the fewest parameters.
M5b rules out the CC-singleton hypothesis — CC's unique variance appears structurally diffuse, not a simple latent construct.
omega_h stability across M3/M4/M5 (0.942→0.939→0.938) confirms g-PSQ robustness regardless of specific-factor specification.
Diminishing returns assessment accepted: remaining RMSEA above 0.10 reflects CC diffuse variance + N-sensitivity, neither resolvable by structural modification.
Correlated-factors model not pursued — would improve fit at the cost of construct-separability claims, without theoretical justification.

cc_construct_question.status: OPEN — deferred to expert validation study
cc_construct_question.reframed_question: Does CC measure a coherent construct, or does it tap multiple situationally-specific processes that share only g-variance?
cc_construct_question.evidence_summary: CC shows 51% pile-up (even with Sonnet), near-zero criterion validity (CMV p=0.155), largest residual across all models (1.275 in M5), and cc_f singleton adds zero fit improvement. Collectively, these suggest CC's unique content does not form a unitary latent dimension.
cc_construct_question.action: Include CC prominently in expert validation study design. If human raters also show high disagreement or pile-up on CC, that confirms a construct-level problem rather than an LLM scoring artifact.

da_singleton_note.observation: DA remains the only dimension with a substantive singleton factor (da_f var=0.129, omega_s=0.067). Combined with the revised paradox interpretation (DA g-loading=0.814 in M5, criterion superiority from content specificity + g signal), DA emerges as the most psychometrically distinctive dimension in the PSQ — strong general safety signal AND meaningful unique peer-context content.
da_singleton_note.implication: DA should receive priority attention in the expert validation study. If human raters confirm DA's unique variance, DA becomes the strongest candidate for a standalone subscale score alongside g-PSQ.

b5_work_stream_close.deliverables_archived: Factor loadings (unstandardized + standardized), fit indices (M3/M4/M5/M5b), omega coefficients, misfit analysis, CC construct evidence, DA paradox revision — all in transport session turns 34-38.
b5_work_stream_close.documentation_target: Psychology-agent will update architecture.md with the M5 structural model as a resolved design decision.

B4_partial_correlations.note: Work order sent turn 22. Independent of B5 work stream. B5 results now provide context: partial correlations controlling for g should show DA retaining the most criterion-relevant unique variance, consistent with da_f omega_s=0.067. B4 results will provide criterion-side evidence complementing B5's structural-side evidence.

claims.text: M5 accepted as the final bifactor structural model for the PSQ. No further structural refinement planned unless human expert validation data reveals a different factor structure.
claims.confidence_basis: 4-way model comparison (M3/M4/M5/M5b) provides clear evidence for M5 on parsimony and fit. Remaining uncertainty: all models fitted on Sonnet LLM labels only. Human expert ratings could reveal different structure.

claims.text: CC's unique variance appears structurally diffuse — not a unitary latent construct. cc_f singleton adds zero fit improvement (chi2 identical to M5). CC's construct validity remains an open question for expert validation.
claims.confidence_basis: M5b empirically rules out simple singleton explanation. 'Structurally diffuse' is an inference from the null cc_f result — alternative explanations include: CC measures a coherent construct that the LLM scorer fails to capture consistently, or CC's content overlaps with multiple other dimensions in ways the bifactor cannot represent.

action_gate.gate_note: B5 work stream complete. No pending gates on structural modeling. B4 partial correlations remain the only open psq-scoring work order.

epistemic_flags: M5 acceptance rests on LLM-derived factor structure (N=4,432 Sonnet labels, 65.4% complete-case subset). Human expert validation may reveal different structure — M5 acceptance carries the qualifier 'best current structural description pending expert validation.'
The chi2 decrease from M4 to M5 (−102.7 with +2 dof) remains a methodological anomaly attributable to numerical instability. M5's superiority rests more reliably on RMSEA comparison than on chi2.

DA as 'most psychometrically distinctive dimension' is a characterization that could change if B4 partial correlations show a different dimension retaining more criterion-relevant unique variance. The structural evidence (omega_s) and criterion evidence (partial r) may not agree.
